Title: The GmSTF1/2–GmBBX4 negative feedback loop acts downstream of blue-light photoreceptors to regulate isoflavonoid biosynthesis in soybean

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR gmcry1s-qm seeds showed significantly reduced total isoflavonoid content. 2. Daidzein, genistein, daidzin, and genistin were reduced in gmcry1s-qm seeds. 3. GmCRY1s positively regulate transcription of GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2 through the downstream GmSTF module.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant biotic/abiotic stress responses; beneficial to human health including anti-osteoporosis, anti-cardiovascular disease, anti-obesity, and anti-cancer effects
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavonoid accumulation in soybean seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; seed metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR of downstream target genes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Positive upstream target for increasing seed isoflavonoid content, but pleiotropic light-response effects should be considered
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of GmSTF1/2 based on this and cited prior work; family analyzed as higher-order mutant rather than single gene resolution

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gmcry2s-tm seeds had significantly reduced total isoflavonoid content. 2. Daidzein, genistein, daidzin, and genistin were reduced in gmcry2s-tm seeds. 3. GmCRY2s positively regulate transcription of GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2 through the downstream GmSTF module.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant biotic/abiotic stress responses; beneficial to human health including anti-osteoporosis, anti-cardiovascular disease, anti-obesity, and anti-cancer effects
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavonoid accumulation in soybean seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; seed metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR of downstream target genes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No chlorophyll change reported under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate positive regulator for isoflavonoid biofortification via light signaling engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of GmSTF1/2 based on this and cited prior work; family analyzed as higher-order mutant rather than single gene resolution

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gmphot1s-tm seeds had lower total isoflavonoid content. 2. Daidzin and genistin were reduced in mutant seeds. 3. GmPHOT1s promote GmSTF protein accumulation and positively regulate GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2 expression.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; immunoblotting of GmSTF proteins; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No chlorophyll change reported under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream signaling component for enhancing seed isoflavonoids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts mainly at protein accumulation level of GmSTFs; mutant is triple mutant family-level analysis

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gmphot2s-dm seeds had lower total isoflavonoid content. 2. Daidzin and genistin were reduced in mutant seeds. 3. GmPHOT2s promote GmSTF protein accumulation and positively regulate GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2 expression.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; immunoblotting of GmSTF proteins; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No chlorophyll change reported under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream signaling component for enhancing seed isoflavonoids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts mainly at protein accumulation level of GmSTFs; mutant is double mutant family-level analysis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase COP1 family
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gmcop1b seeds accumulated significantly more total isoflavonoids and several glycosylated/malonylated isoflavonoids than wild type. 2. GmCOP1b interacts with GmSTF1 and promotes lower GmSTF abundance in the light. 3. GmCOP1b acts as a negative regulator of isoflavonoid biosynthesis downstream of photoreceptors.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates isoflavonoid biosynthesis; loss of function increases seed isoflavonoids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; seed metabolite profiling; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation imaging; co-immunoprecipitation; immunoblotting
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acts in light signaling; may affect photomorphogenesis-related traits
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A negative regulator whose editing could increase seed isoflavonoid content, but broad light-development functions require caution
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmCOP1a showed overlap in dark GmSTF degradation but did not affect seed isoflavonoids under tested conditions

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream phenylpropanoid entry step feeding isoflavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes non-oxidative deamination of L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate and ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation by GmSTF1/2 is associated with promotion of isoflavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves and immature seeds; reduced in gmstfs-dm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; light signaling via photoreceptors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmPAL2.1 native promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be targeted with upstream regulatory engineering to enhance precursor supply for seed isoflavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct GmSTF1/2 binding requires the second ACE motif in the promoter

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream phenylpropanoid entry step feeding isoflavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes non-oxidative deamination of L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate and ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation by GmSTF1/2 is associated with promotion of isoflavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves and immature seeds; reduced in gmstfs-dm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; light signaling via photoreceptors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmPAL2.3 native promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct target gene for enhancing precursor supply into isoflavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmSTF1/2 binding requires a promoter G-box motif; GmBBX4 inhibits GmSTF-mediated transactivation on this promoter

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosylation of isoflavone aglycones daidzein, genistein, and glycitein to corresponding glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation by GmSTF1/2 promotes accumulation of glycosylated isoflavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves and immature seeds; reduced in gmstfs-dm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; light signaling via photoreceptors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmUGT2 native promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering glucoside-rich isoflavonoid profiles in seed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of 18 GmUGT genes regulated by GmSTF1/2, but only GmUGT2 was directly validated

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of biosynthetic genes and transcriptional repression of GmBBX4; protein stability control by photoreceptors/COP1b
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with GmBBX4; interacts with GmCOP1b
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2; represses GmBBX4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator acting downstream of blue-light photoreceptors to promote isoflavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein accumulates in leaves under light; transgene increases seed isoflavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; long-day conditions; light versus dark affects protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant and overexpression analyses; seed metabolite profiling; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; LCI; co-IP; immunoblotting
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong positive engineering target for high-isoflavonoid soybean; stacking with downstream pathway genes may be effective
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic roles in photomorphogenesis, chlorophyll accumulation, GA metabolism, and nodulation reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmSTF1 and GmSTF2 act redundantly; together regulate ~29 of 104 isoflavonoid-related genes

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of biosynthetic genes and transcriptional repression of GmBBX4; protein stability control by photoreceptors/COP1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with GmBBX4; interacts with GmCOP1a
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2; represses GmBBX4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator acting redundantly with GmSTF1 downstream of blue-light photoreceptors
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein accumulates in leaves under light; transgene increases seed isoflavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; long-day conditions; light versus dark affects protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant and overexpression analyses; seed metabolite profiling; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; LCI; co-IP; immunoblotting
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong positive engineering target for improving seed isoflavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic roles in photomorphogenesis, GA metabolism, and nodulation reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmSTF2 EMSA on GmBBX4 promoter showed two shifted bands, suggesting possible homodimer binding in vitro

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein inhibition of GmSTF1/2 transcriptional activation activity; negative regulation within a feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box protein with two B-box domains and one CCT domain
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmSTF1/2 target-gene activation module affecting GmPAL2.1; GmPAL2.3; GmUGT2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses GmSTF1/2-mediated activation of GmPAL2.1, GmPAL2.3, and GmUGT2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator that dampens GmSTF-driven isoflavonoid biosynthesis in a negative feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant stress responses and human health-promoting nutraceutical compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates isoflavonoid biosynthesis; knockout increases and overexpression decreases seed isoflavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript repressed by GmSTF1/2; overexpression lines show high transcript and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Forms a negative feedback loop with GmSTF1/2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant and overexpression analyses; seed metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation imaging; co-IP; yeast one-hybrid-based inhibition assay; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout/editing of this negative regulator is a promising route to elevate seed isoflavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Also negatively regulates light signaling and affects hypocotyl elongation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: May also inhibit isoflavonoid biosynthesis partly independently of GmSTF1/2, as noted by the authors
